question.text: If a NIFTY 25,000 call option is bought at Rs 300 and NIFTY expires at 25,500, what is the profit per lot (lot size 25)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Gross payoff = max(25,500 - 25,000, 0) = 500. Net profit = 500 - 300 (premium) = 200 per unit. Per lot = 200 × 25 = Rs 5,000. **The Trap:** Rs 12,500 uses gross payoff (500 × 25) without subtracting premium. Rs 7,500 subtracts only half the premium. Rs 2,500 uses wrong lot size. **Cross-Exam Context:** Option P&L calculations are fundamental across all markets. NIFTY lot size of 25 is standard. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The Rs 300 premium cost = Rs 7,500 per lot (300 × 25). A Rs 5,000 profit on Rs 7,500 investment = 67% return. If NIFTY stayed below 25,000, the entire Rs 7,500 would be lost. This illustrates options' leveraged, asymmetric payoff.
